fragility_points: A US-morning risk-off move on April 17 could push BTC back below 74000 at the exact settlement minute.
Exchange-specific or timing-specific noise matters more here than in broader daily-close markets.
key_assumptions: No major negative macro or crypto-specific shock hits before noon ET on April 17.
BTC stays roughly in the current 74k-75k regime into the settlement window.
Binance BTCUSDT remains representative enough of broader spot into settlement.
main_logical_chain: The governing contract resolves on Binance BTCUSDT 12:00 ET one-minute close, strictly above 74000.
Current Binance spot is already modestly above 74000 and recent venue data shows BTC trading in that regime.
That makes Yes the slight favorite, but only by a small margin because a normal intraday downtick near noon could still settle No.
main_thesis: BTC is slightly more likely than not to settle above 74000 because spot is already above the threshold, but the edge is fragile since the contract resolves on a single noon ET Binance minute.
quote_anchors: Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone
final Close price higher than the price specified
source_quality_view: Good enough for a medium-difficulty run: primary contract rules plus direct venue data, with one independent contextual price cross-check.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on a single one-minute Binance close at noon ET.
Current spot is only modestly above the threshold, so routine volatility can flip the outcome.
strongest_supports: Binance spot was already above 74000 at research time, around 74793.86.
Recent Binance daily closes show BTC has recently traded and closed around the target zone.
CoinGecko cross-check suggests Binance was not showing an obviously idiosyncratic print.
timing_relevance: Very high because the contract depends on a single Binance one-minute noon ET close rather than a daily close or broad BTC level.
unresolved_ambiguities: No specific scheduled catalyst was identified as dominant; the main risk is an unscheduled or broad macro repricing event.
Narrow timestamped settlement always leaves some practical candle-labeling sensitivity even when the rules are explicit.
what_would_change_view: A decisive loss of 74000 before settlement or a credible negative macro/crypto shock would move the view toward No; holding comfortably above about 74.5k-75k near noon would raise confidence in Yes.
